title: Evelina
description: Evelina — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: "Queen Ann Street"
memes.description: "The Mirvans' London residence on Queen Ann Street — Evelina's home during her first London season and the setting for key domestic scenes"

memes.label: "The Assembly"
memes.description: "The formal public dance gatherings that structure eighteenth-century social life in Evelina — arenas of courtship, class judgment, and moral testing"

memes.label: Assembly Rooms
memes.description: Assembly rooms in Frances Burney's Evelina — the 18th-century public venues for balls and social gatherings central to the novel's courtship plot.

memes.label: "Bath"
memes.description: "The most fashionable spa city of Georgian England — mentioned in Evelina as a destination of health and social display"

memes.label: "Berry Hill"
memes.description: "Reverend Villars's country estate in Dorsetshire — Evelina's childhood home and the novel's moral anchor, representing tranquillity, virtue, and refuge from London's corruption"

memes.label: Breeding
memes.description: Good breeding in Frances Burney's Evelina — the 18th-century concept encompassing upbringing, manners, and social formation as markers of class and character.

memes.label: "Bristol Hotwells"
memes.description: "The spa resort near Bristol where the latter part of Evelina takes place — site of her reunion with her father, Sir John Belmont"

memes.label: "Captain Mirvan"
memes.description: "A blunt, unpolished naval captain whose practical jokes and abuse of Madame Duval provide much of the novel's dark comedy — husband of Mrs. Mirvan and father of Maria"

memes.label: Clifton
memes.description: Clifton, an elevated spa suburb near Bristol, as a setting in Frances Burney's Evelina — a site of scenic beauty, social visits, and fashionable resort life.

memes.label: "Clifton Hill"
memes.description: "The elegant Bristol residence of Mrs. Beaumont on Clifton Hill, where key revelations of the novel's final act occur"

memes.label: Compassion
memes.description: Compassion and benevolence in Frances Burney's Evelina — the moral sentiment that binds characters and signals virtue in an 18th-century sentimental novel.

memes.label: "Conduct"
memes.description: "The central moral concept of Evelina — encompassing behaviour, manners, and ethical character, conduct is the standard by which every character in Burney's novel is measured"

memes.label: "White-Conduit House"
memes.description: "A minor London pleasure garden and tea-house chosen by the Branghton party — representing the vulgar entertainments of the middling sort"

memes.label: "Delicacy"
memes.description: "A key term in the moral vocabulary of Evelina — denoting fineness of perception, moral sensitivity, and the capacity to be affected by beauty and goodness"

memes.label: "Drury Lane Theatre"
memes.description: "The Theatre Royal Drury Lane — where Evelina sees David Garrick perform and first experiences the enchantment of London theatre"

memes.label: England
memes.description: The primary setting of the novel — the country to which Evelina travels and where most of the action unfolds

memes.label: "Evelina Anville"
memes.description: "The protagonist of Fanny Burney's epistolary novel — a young woman of beauty, innocence, and sensibility navigating London society under the guardianship of Rev. Villars"

memes.label: "Fanny Burney"
memes.description: "Frances 'Fanny' Burney (1752–1840) — the author of Evelina (1778), one of the most important novelists of the eighteenth century and a pioneering voice in the development of the novel of manners"

memes.label: "Fantoccini"
memes.description: "A puppet-show entertainment in London, combining French and Italian comedy, which Evelina attends during her first London season"

memes.label: Fashion
memes.description: Fashion and fashionable society in Frances Burney's Evelina — how style, appearances, and the beau monde shape social experience in 18th-century London.

memes.label: Fortune
memes.description: The role of fortune, wealth, and inheritance in Frances Burney's Evelina — a social determinant shaping marriage prospects, social standing, and personal fate.

memes.label: Gallantry
memes.description: Gallantry in Frances Burney's Evelina — the 18th-century code of male social attention that ranges from polite compliment to predatory pursuit.

memes.label: Hampstead
memes.description: A village north of London where assemblies and public entertainments were held, visited by Evelina and her party

memes.label: "Hampstead Ball"
memes.description: "A public ball at the long room in Hampstead, where Evelina endures the vulgarity of her Branghton relations and reflects on her earlier ballroom experiences"

memes.label: Holborn
memes.description: A commercial London district where Evelina lodges with the Branghton family during her second stay in London

memes.label: "Honour"
memes.description: "The code of personal integrity, social reputation, and moral standing in 18th-century English society as depicted in Fanny Burney's Evelina"

memes.label: "Bristol Hotwells"
memes.description: "The spa at Bristol where the novel's climactic sequences unfold — a fashionable resort for health and society"

memes.label: "Howard Grove"
memes.description: "The country estate of Lady Howard and the Mirvan family — the site of Evelina's first introduction to society beyond Berry Hill"

memes.label: Kensington Gardens
memes.description: Kensington Gardens in Frances Burney's Evelina — a fashionable London park for promenading and social display.

memes.label: Lady Belmont
memes.description: Deceased mother of Evelina, formerly Caroline Evelyn; daughter of Mr. Evelyn, ward of Madame Duval, married to Sir John Belmont; her deathbed entrustment of Evelina to Rev. Villars sets the novel's plot in motion.

memes.label: "Lady Howard"
memes.description: "The dignified matriarch of Howard Grove who initiates Evelina's introduction to society and mediates between Mr. Villars and Madame Duval"

memes.label: "Lady Louisa Larpent"
memes.description: "Lord Orville's affected and languid sister; a fashionable young lady whose vanity and affectation make her a foil to Evelina's natural sensibility"

memes.label: "London"
memes.description: "The primary urban setting of Evelina — a city of fashion, danger, and social opportunity in the 18th century"

memes.label: "Lord Merton"
memes.description: "A dissolute nobleman engaged to Lady Louisa Larpent; his callous behaviour — culminating in the old women's race — exposes the moral bankruptcy of fashionable libertinism in Burney's novel"

memes.label: "Lord Orville"
memes.description: "The ideal gentleman and romantic hero of Fanny Burney's Evelina — courteous, principled, and the moral standard of the novel"

memes.label: "Madame Duval"
memes.description: "Evelina's French grandmother — vulgar, tempestuous, and unprincipled — whose sudden arrival in England throws the young heroine's life into turmoil"

memes.label: "Marybone Gardens Visit"
memes.description: "An evening at Marybone Gardens featuring a violin concerto and a firework display of Orpheus and Eurydice, during which Evelina becomes separated from her party and endures a terrifying encounter"

memes.label: "Miss Branghton"
memes.description: "The eldest Branghton daughter; proud, ill-tempered, and conceited, she resents Evelina's gentility and pines for Mr. Smith, the family's lodger"

memes.label: "Miss Mirvan"
memes.description: "Maria Mirvan, daughter of Captain and Mrs. Mirvan, is Evelina's closest friend and confidante throughout her introduction to London society"

memes.label: "Miss Polly"
memes.description: "The younger daughter of Mr. Branghton the silversmith; romantic and naive, she pursues Mr. Smith as her 'lover' while competing with her elder sister Biddy for masculine attention"

memes.label: "Modesty"
memes.description: "A cardinal feminine virtue in the moral world of Evelina — the quality of self-effacement that protects a young woman's reputation and signals her virtue to the world"

memes.label: "Monsieur Du Bois"
memes.description: "Madame Duval's French companion in Burney's Evelina; a figure of comic misfortune caught between two cultures"

memes.label: "Mr. Branghton"
memes.description: "A silversmith of Snow Hill, Holborn; Madame Duval's nephew and Evelina's reluctant London host, embodying the narrow prejudices of the City tradesman"

memes.label: "Mr. Brown"
memes.description: "A young man of the Branghtons' social circle; he eventually marries Miss Polly Branghton and is notable chiefly for reporting Mr. Smith's declaration against matrimony"

memes.label: "Mr. Coverley"
memes.description: "A minor nobleman and companion of Lord Merton at Clifton; known for his reckless driving, crude wit, and participation in the degrading 'old women's race' wager"

memes.label: Mr. Evelyn
memes.description: Grandfather of Evelina and father of Caroline Evelyn (Lady Belmont); a gentleman who travelled with Rev. Villars as his tutor and named Villars sole guardian of his daughter; his estate is later disputed by Madame Duval.

memes.label: "Mr. Lovel"
memes.description: "A foppish and impertinent suitor in Burney's Evelina; emblem of fashionable London society's affectation and malice"

memes.label: "Mr. Macartney"
memes.description: "A melancholy young Scotsman lodging with the Branghtons, whose secret history — involving poverty, a lost love, and a near-tragedy — constitutes the novel's most elaborate subplot"

memes.label: "Mr. Smith"
memes.description: "A lodger at the Branghtons' house in Holborn; a would-be fine gentleman who courts Miss Polly Branghton while Miss Biddy Branghton pines for him"

memes.label: "Mrs. Beaumont"
memes.description: "A fashionable hostess at Clifton Hill, Bristol; a relation of Lord Orville whose house becomes the setting for the novel's climactic social encounters"

memes.label: Mrs. Clinton
memes.description: Rev. Villars's housekeeper and Evelina's former nurse; a loyal, "most worthy" attendant who accompanies Evelina on journeys and plays a key role in uncovering the truth about Sir John Belmont's deceived acknowledgment.

memes.label: "Mrs. Mirvan"
memes.description: "The kind-hearted hostess who chaperones Evelina to London; a model of maternal warmth and social grace in Burney's novel"

memes.label: "Mrs. Selwyn"
memes.description: "A sharp-witted, plain-speaking gentlewoman who accompanies Evelina to Bristol and Clifton; an unconventional female intellect who challenges social pretensions"

memes.label: "Old Women's Race"
memes.description: "A grotesque wager settled by a race between two elderly women at Clifton, exposing the brutal frivolity of fashionable gaming culture"

memes.label: "The Opera"
memes.description: "The Italian opera at the Haymarket — London's most fashionable theatrical entertainment, serving in Evelina as a setting for social display, romantic competition, and the theatre of class"

memes.label: "Pantheon"
memes.description: "The grand assembly rooms on Oxford Street — the last public diversion Evelina attends in London before departing for Bristol, and a space that inspires awe rather than gaiety"

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The French capital, representing fashion, refinement, and cultural polish in the novel

memes.label: Politeness
memes.description: Politeness and manners in Frances Burney's Evelina — the 18th-century code of civility that governs social interaction and reveals character.

memes.label: "Private Ball"
memes.description: "Evelina's first ball — a private ball given by Mrs. Stanley, where she experiences both the pleasures and perils of fashionable society"

memes.label: "Propriety"
memes.description: "The social code governing acceptable behaviour in eighteenth-century England — in Evelina, propriety is both a genuine moral safeguard and a weapon of class exclusion"

memes.label: Prudence
memes.description: Prudence as a moral virtue in Frances Burney's Evelina — the exercise of caution, discretion, and sound judgment in navigating social dangers.

memes.label: Queen Ann Street
memes.description: A London street where Mrs. Mirvan's residence is located, serving as Evelina's base in the capital

memes.label: "Ranelagh"
memes.description: "The fashionable London pleasure gardens in Chelsea — a glittering social stage where Evelina navigates the dangers of public scrutiny, unwanted attention, and class performance"

memes.label: Rank
memes.description: Social rank and hierarchy in Frances Burney's Evelina — how birth, title, and station shape character interactions and social mobility in 18th-century England.

memes.label: "Reputation"
memes.description: "Social standing and public estimation of character in 18th-century England as depicted in Evelina"

memes.label: "Rev. Arthur Villars"
memes.description: "The pious and prudent guardian of Evelina — a country clergyman whose moral wisdom guides the novel's ethical framework from his retirement at Berry Hill"

memes.label: "The Ridotto"
memes.description: "A pivotal social event in Evelina where the heroine faces public embarrassment and social peril"

memes.label: Sentiment
memes.description: Sentiment and the culture of sensibility in Frances Burney's Evelina — the 18th-century elevation of feeling as a moral and aesthetic faculty.

memes.label: "Sir Clement Willoughby"
memes.description: "A charming but unscrupulous baronet who pursues Evelina with calculated persistence — the novel's most skilled manipulator and Lord Orville's foil"

memes.label: "Sir John Belmont"
memes.description: "A dissolute baronet; the biological father of Evelina, who secretly married Lady Belmont, burned the marriage certificate, and denied the union — the central antagonist of the novel's parentage plot"

memes.label: "Snow Hill"
memes.description: "A commercial street near Holborn where the Branghton family keeps a silversmith's shop — representing the world of London trade"

memes.label: "Social Class"
memes.description: "The rigid hierarchy of rank that structures the world of Evelina — a system the novel both accepts and critiques through its heroine's ambiguous social position"

memes.label: "Theatre"
memes.description: "The theatrical outings in Evelina — including a performance of King Lear at Drury Lane — as occasions for social display, emotional education, and dangerous encounters"

memes.label: Vanity
memes.description: Vanity as a moral theme in Frances Burney's Evelina — the comic and moral dimensions of excessive self-regard in 18th-century society.

memes.label: "Vauxhall Gardens"
memes.description: "The famous London pleasure gardens — a backdrop for key social encounters in Evelina and one of the most celebrated entertainment venues of Georgian England"

memes.label: "Virtue"
memes.description: "A central moral concept in Evelina — the quality of moral excellence that Rev. Villars cultivates in his ward and that the novel's social world continually tests"
